name: pyrfume

metadata.name: pyrfume
metadata.summary: A validation library for human olfactory psychophysics research.

documentation:
# Pyrfume

![Pyrfume logo](https://avatars3.githubusercontent.com/u/34174393?s=200&v=4)

#### `pyrfume` is a python library for olfactory psychophysics research. See "notebooks" for examples of use.
[![Python package](https://github.com/pyrfume/pyrfume/actions/workflows/pythonpackage.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/pyrfume/pyrfume/actions/workflows/pythonpackage.yml)
[![Travis](https://travis-ci.org/pyrfume/pyrfume.svg?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.org/pyrfume/pyrfume) 
[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/pyrfume/pyrfume/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://coveralls.io/github/pyrfume/pyrfume?branch=master)
![Zenodo](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/549787/165869234-79bf95db-0b6c-495c-a1a8-b3db751f3352.png)


### Examples:
```
# Load data for Snitz et al, 2013 (PLOS Computational Biology)
import pyrfume
behavior = pyrfume.load_data('snitz_2013/behavior.csv')
molecules = pyrfume.load_data('snitz_2013/molecules.csv')

# Load data for Bushdid et al, 2014 (Science)
import pyrfume
behavior = pyrfume.load_data('bushdid_2014/behavior.csv')
molecules = pyrfume.load_data('bushdid_2014/molecules.csv')
stimuli = pyrfume.load_data('bushdid_2014/stimuli.csv')
```

### Contributing

Just run `./develop.sh` to get started with developing `pyrfume`.

### [Website](http://pyrfume.org)

### [Data Repository](https://github.com/pyrfume/pyrfume-data)

### [Paper](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2022.09.08.507170)

### [Data Curation Status](http://status.pyrfume.org)

### [Docs](http://docs.pyrfume.org)

*Licensing/Copyright*: Data is provided as-is.  Licensing information for individual datasets is available in the data repository.  Takedown requests for datasets may be directed to admin at pyrfume dot org.

modules.name: pyrfume.dravnieks
documentation:
dravieks.txt produced by copying and pasting from the supplemental material of
"In search of the structure of human olfactory space", e.g. koulakov.pdf.

functions.name: pyrfume.loaders.manoel_2016.get_translater
documentation: Load data about the odorants (names and PubChem IDs)

functions.name: pyrfume.optimization.get_coverage
documentation:
This function will be used to compute coverage of odorant space during
optimization. We want non-selected odorants to be 'covered' as much as
possible by selected ones

functions.name: pyrfume.optimization.get_spacing
documentation:
These function will be used to determine the spacing between the
selected odorants in the low-d manifold. We don't want to select odorants
which are right next to each other in odorant space

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    library (pd.DataFrame): A pandas dataframe containing odorants and
        their attributes. The index should contain chemical identifiers
        (e.g. CIDs). The column names should be attributes, e.g.
        molecular weight, price, etc.  They can also be descriptors.
    n_desired (int): The size of the set of odorants desired from this
        library.
    keep (list): An iterable of library index values that must exist in every
        candidate set. Obviously this must be less than `n_desired`.
    weights (list): A list of 3-tuples each containing the name of a
        weight, a method to apply to the library dataframe, and a
        weight to apply to the function's output.
    fitness: A fitness class from DEAP
    mu (int): Size of the population (number of odorant sets under
        consideration in each generation).
    lamda (int): ...
    p_cx (float): Crossover probability in each generation.
    p_mut (float): Mutation probability in each generation.
    sel: Selection algorithm, e.g. 'selBest' or 'selNSGA2'
    standardize_weights (bool): Interpret weights as multiples of stdev
        of a weight function

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.compute_weight_stats
documentation:
Compute scores for a bunch of random individuals to use for
standardizing fitness scores and thus making weights interpretable
as # of sd's better or worse than random individuals

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.eval_individual
documentation:
Evaluate the fitness of the odorant set.

Args:
    ind: A DEAP individual as registered above.
    standardize (bool, optional): Whether to standardize the
        resulting score.

Returns:
    A fitness score.

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.crossover
documentation:
Apply a crossover operation on input sets.

Args:
    ind1: The first individual
    ind2: The second individual

Returns:
    tuple: Two new individuals

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.mutate
documentation:
Mutation that pops or adds some elements.

Args:
    individual: An individual

Returns:
    tuple: A new individual (wrapped in a tuple)

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.force_keep
documentation:
Ensure that the library indices in `self.keep` get included

Args:
    ind: An individual.

Returns:
    An individual with certain additional items kept.

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.run
documentation:
Run the optimization

Args:
    pop: The population to start with.
    hof: The Hall of Fame to start with
    quiet: (bool, optional): Whether to suppress stdout.
        Defaults to False.

Returns:
    tuple: The population, stats, Hall of Fame, and logboox

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.eval_mean
documentation:
Evaluation the mean of column for the selected individual

Args:
    individual: An individual.
    column (str): A column name from the library dataframe.

Returns:
    float: The mean of that column, for the subset
        described by the individual

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.eval_sum
documentation:
Evaluation the sum of column for the selected individual

Args:
    individual: An individual.
    column (str): A column name from the library dataframe.

Returns:
    float: The sum of that column, for the subset
        described by the individual

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.dask_map
documentation:
A mapping function for Dask.  Used for multithreading.

Args:
    f: Any function.
    x (list): A list of inputs to be sequentially passed
        to that function.

Returns:
    list: A list of outputs from that function.

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.plot_score_history
documentation: Plot the history of each of the scores over generations.

methods.name: pyrfume.optimization.OdorantSetOptimizer.embed_gene_2d
documentation: size: a tuple of base size and a multiplier for values in the size column

from Supplemental Table 2 of Bushdid et al.
Functions for loading data from Bushdid et al, 2014.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.__init__
documentation: Builds odorant from a list of components.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.N
documentation: Number of components in this odorant.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.r
documentation: Number of replacements (swaps) to get from self to another odorant.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.overlap
documentation:
Overlap between self and another odorant.  Complement of r.
Optionally report result as percent relative to number of components.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.hamming
documentation:
Hamming distance between self and another odorant.
Synonymous with number of d, the number of total 'moves' to go from
one odorant to another.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.add_component
documentation: Adds one component to an odorant.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.remove_component
documentation: Removes one component to an odorant.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.descriptor_list
documentation: Given a data source, returns a list of descriptors about this odorant.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.descriptor_vector
documentation:
Given a data source, returns a vector of descriptors about this
odorant. The vector will contain positive floats.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.descriptor_vector2
documentation:
Returns a vector of descriptors about this odorant, combining multiple
data sources.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.described_components
documentation:
Given a data source, returns a list of the components which are
described by that source, i.e. those that have descriptors.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.n_described_components
documentation:
Given a data source, returns the number of components that are
described by that data source.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.fraction_components_described
documentation:
Given a data source, returns the fraction of components that are
described by that data source.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.__str__
documentation: String representation of the odorant.

class_variables.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.name
documentation: Name of odorant, built from a hash of component names.

class_variables.name: pyrfume.objects.Mixture.C
documentation: Number of components from which to choose.

A mixture of molecules, defined by the presence of absence of the
candidate molecules in the mixture.

classes.name: pyrfume.objects.Component

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Component.__init__
documentation:
Components are defined by a component_id from the Bushdid et al
supplemental material, a name, a CAS number, a percent dilution,
and a solvent.

methods.name: pyrfume.objects.Component.set_descriptors
documentation:
Given a data source, sets descriptors for this odorant using
a dictionary where CAS numbers are keys, and descriptors are values.
A single molecule, which may or may not be present in an odorant.

Create negative integer hash for single molecules with a known structure

methods.name: pyrfume.odorants.Solution.molecules

methods.name: pyrfume.odorants.Solution.mole_fractions

methods.name: pyrfume.odorants.Solution.partial_pressures
documentation:
Computes partial pressures for each odorant
in the mixture using Raoult's law

methods.name: pyrfume.odorants.Solution.total_pressure
documentation: Computes total pressure of the vapor using Dalton's law

methods.name: pyrfume.odorants.Solution.vapor_concentrations
documentation:
Concentrations of each component in the vapor phase at steady state.
Units are fraction of volume. Air is assumed to make up the balance

methods.name: pyrfume.odorants.Compound.__getattr__
documentation:
If no attribute is found, try looking up on the
ChemicalOrder or the Molecule

modules.name: pyrfume.benchmarking
documentation: Pyrfume benchmarking module.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.resolve_feature_selection
documentation: Generator feature selection transformation.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.resolve_task
documentation: Determine whether classification or regression task.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.get_default_parameters
documentation: Return default parameter grid for specified estimator.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.resolve_scoring
documentation:
Return scoring metrics for GridSearchCV.

Args:
    scoring: Scoring metric(s) for GridSearchCV. If None, uses 'task' to return defaults.
    task: Specify 'classification' or 'regression'.

Returns:
    Either single or list of scoring metric(s).

Raises:
    ValueError: If scoring is None, and task is not 'classification' or 'regression'.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.pipeline_steps_to_string
documentation: Create string from steps in pipeline.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.list_default_estimators
documentation: Return list of estimators that are provided with default parameter grids.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.get_molecule_features
documentation:
Fetches specified features for molecule CIDs in 'index'.

Mordred and Morgan features have already been scaled (StandardScaler) and imputed
    (KNNImputer) at the time of creation in the Pyrfume-Data repository. Here during retrieval,
    features will zero variance will be dropped.

Args:
    index: molecule CIDs for which to fetch features.
    feature_set: Use 'mordred' for Mordred features, 'morgan' for Morgan similarity, or
        'mordred_morgan' to merge these two feature sets.

Returns:
    DataFrame of the specified feature set, indexed on CID.

Raises:
    ValueError: if feature_set is not 'morgan', 'mordred', or 'mordred_morgan'.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.resolve_cv
documentation: Return cross-validation generator for classification or regresson task.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.get_train_test_splits
documentation:
Returns indices for train/test splits created by cross-validation generator.

Args:
    dataset: PyrfumeDataset instance containing prediction target and molecule features.

Returns:
    List of train/test indices for each fold created by cross-validation generator. Format is a
        list of length = n_splits, each list elment is a tuple = (train indices, test indices).

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.reformat_param_grid
documentation:
Reformat parameter grid for GridSearchCV expecting Pipeline.

Reformats Model.param_gird from dict of dicts to flat dict with convention:
{step_name__param_name: param_values}

Args:
    parameters: dict of parameter grid for pipeline steps.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.evaluate_model
documentation:
Perfoms GridSearchCV on specified classifier or regressor pipeline.

Args:
    dataset: PyrfumeDataset instance containing prediction target and molecule features.
    pipeline: Model instance specifies pipeline steps, prameter grid, and scoring.
    verbose: Indicates level of display output.

Returns:
    GridSearchCV instance.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.pipeline_from_string_list
documentation: Reconstructs a pipeline from a string.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.gridsearch_csv_to_frame
documentation:
Converts gridsearch results .csv file into DataFrame with Pipelines in
df['pipeline_steps'].

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.evaluate_dummy_model
documentation:
Provides baseline results on dummy models for regression and classification.

Args:
    dataset: PyrfumeDataset instance containing prediction target and molecule features.
    scoring: Scoring metric(s) to use. If None, will use ML task to get defaults.

Returns:
    DataFrame with rows = strategies, cols = scoring.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.gridsearch_results_to_dataframe
documentation: Parse GridSearchCV results into DataFrame for easier downstream handling.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.remove_prefix
documentation: For Python 3.8 compatibitity.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.get_best_results
documentation:
Filter out best scores and parameters for estimator pipelines.

Args:
    resutls: GridSearchCV results from gridsearch_results_to_dataframe().
    metric: Scoring metric. If None, all scores and parameters are returned.
    single_best: Option to return single best result from all estimator pipelines.
        Only valid if a scoring metric is provided.
    include_pipeline_steps: Option to return list of steps in estimator pipeline.

Returns:
    Dataframe of 'best' resutls from GridSearchCV.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.verify_batch_settings
documentation:
Verify that prediction target/feature set cobminations return valid datasets.

Args:
    archive: Pyrfume-Data archive.
    targets: List of prediction targets.
    feature_sets: List of molecule feature types.
    prepare_dataset: Function that creates pipeline compatible dataset.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.simualate_batch_results
documentation: Simulates results from a batch GridSearchCV for testing purposes.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.batch_gridsearchcv
documentation:
Performs GridSearchCV over target, feature, and estimator pipeline permutations.

Args:
    archive: Pyrfume-Data archive.
    targets: List of prediction targets.
    feature_sets: List of molecule feature types.
    pipelines: List of Model instances.
    prepare_dataset: Function that creates pipeline compatible dataset.

Returns:
    DataFrame of top scoring metircs and parameter sets for each combination of prediction
        target, feature set, and estimator pipeline.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.reconstruct_pipeline
documentation:
Reconstruct Pipeline from list of steps and parameter string.

Args:
    pipeline_steps: List of preprossing steps (optional) and estimator (must be last element
        in list).
    param_string: Parameters for estimator in condensed string form.

Returns:
    sklearn Pipeline instance.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.reconstruct_model
documentation:
Reconstruct prediction-ready model.

Must provide either pipeline steps and parameter string to reconstruct a specific model,
or a results dataframe and scoring metric to reconstruct top-scoring model.

Args:
    dataset: PyfumeDataset class instance.
    pipeline_steps: List of steps for pipeline. Last step must be an estimator.
    param_string: Parameters for estimator.
    resutls: Results for best GridSearchCV scoring metrics from either get_best_results()
        or batch_gridsearchcv().
    metric: Scoring metric which determines 'best' estimator.

Returns:
    Prediction-ready Pipeline instance.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.apply_model
documentation:
Test prediction from estimator pipeline.

 Args:
    dataset: PyfumeDataset class instance.
    model: Pre-fitted Pipeline instance.

Returns:
    DataFrame with original prediction targets and predictions.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.plot_heatmap
documentation:
Generate heatmap of top metric scores.

Args:
    results: Results for best GridSearchCV scoring metrics from either get_best_results()
        or batch_gridsearchcv().
    save_fig: If True, heatmap will be saved as 'benchmarks.png'.
    maxima: Can be 'row', 'column', or None (default). Allows for easier inspection of
        leading metrics by model, or leading models by metric.
    show_rect: If True, rectangles are added to identify lead models.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.save_benchmarks
documentation:
Save best metric scores for each pipeline to CSV file.

Args:
    results: Results for best GridSearchCV scoring metrics from either get_best_results()
        or batch_gridsearchcv().
    csv_name: Name for CSV file being saved.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.execute_viz_notebook
documentation: Execute a remote visualization notebook via papermill.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.fit_model_for_pickle
documentation: Function to add fitted model to dataframe.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.pickle_best_models
documentation: Fit model for each model/metric pair and pickle.

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.iterate_and_plot_models
documentation:
Iterates through a dataframe of fitted models and plots performance summaries.

Args:

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.plot_score_report

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.show_roc_curve

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.plot_residuals

functions.name: pyrfume.benchmarking.show_regressor_performance
documentation: Plot residuals and actual v. predicted for a regression model.

functions.name: pyrfume.experiments.odorant_distances
documentation:
Given the test results, returns a dictionary whose keys are odorant pairs
and whose values are psychometric distances between those pairs,
defined as the fraction of discriminations that were incorrect.
This can be limited to one subject indicated by subject_id, or else
by default it pools across all subjects.

functions.name: pyrfume.experiments.ROC
documentation:
Given test results and a number of components N, returns a distribution
of the number of distinct components 'r' for correct trials (right) and
incorrect trials (wrong), in tests using odorants with N total components.
These can later be plotted or used to generated an ROC curve.

functions.name: pyrfume.experiments.correct_matrix
documentation:
Given test results, a number of components N, and a level of overlap
between odorants, returns a num_subjects by num_test matrix of booleans
corresponding to the correctness of that subject's response on that test.

classes.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.__init__
documentation:
Tests are defined by their universal identifier (UID), the 3
odorants used (2 should be identical), the dilution, and the
identity of the correct response, which should be the odd-ball.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.add_odorant
documentation: Adds one odorant to this test.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.add_odorants
documentation: Adds more than one odorants to this test.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.double
documentation: Returns the odorant present twice in this test.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.single
documentation: Returns the odorant present once in this test.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.pair
documentation:
Returns the odorant pair in this test, with the odorant present
twice listed first.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.N
documentation:
Returns the number of components in each of the odorants.
This a single value since they should all have the same number
of components.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.r
documentation:
Returns the number of component replacements (swaps) separating one of
the odorants from the other.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.overlap
documentation:
Returns the overlap (complement of r) between the two odorants.
Optionally returns this as a percentage of N.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.common_components
documentation: Returns a list of components common to the two odorants.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.unique_components
documentation: Returns a list of components that exactly one of the two odorants has.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.unique_descriptors
documentation:
Given a data source, returns a list of descriptors that
exactly one of the two odorants has.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.common_descriptors
documentation:
Given a data source, returns a list of descriptors that
are common to the two odorants.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.descriptors_correlation
documentation:
Given a data source, returns the correlation between the descriptors
of the two odorants.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.descriptors_correlation2
documentation:
Returns the correlation between the descriptors
of the two odorants, combining multiple data sources.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.descriptors_difference
documentation:
Given a data source, returns the absolute difference between the
descriptors of the two odorants.

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.TriangleTest.n_undescribed
documentation:
Given a data source, returns the number of components from among the
two odorants that are not described by that source.

One kind of experimental test, as performed by e.g. Bushdid et al.
A 'triangle test' has three stimuli, two of which are the same two
odorants, and is defined by those odorants.

classes.name: pyrfume.experiments.Result

methods.name: pyrfume.experiments.Result.__init__
documentation:
Results are defined by the test to which they correspond,
the id of the subject taking that test, and whether the subject
gave the correct answer.
A test result, corresponding to one test given to one subject.

classes.name: pyrfume.experiments.Distance
documentation:
An odorant distance, corresponding to distance between two odorants.
No particular implementation for computing distance is mandated.

functions.name: pyrfume.features.load_dragon
documentation:
Loads dragon features.
Use a suffix to specify a precomputed cleaning of this data

functions.name: pyrfume.features.cid_names
documentation: TODO: Fix this to use the larger file

functions.name: pyrfume.features.tanimoto_sim
documentation: Compute Tanimoto similarity for just two molecules.

functions.name: pyrfume.features.get_maximum_tanimoto_similarity
documentation: Compute maximal Tanimoto similarity to `reference_set` for all given molecules.

modules.name: pyrfume.datajoint_tools
documentation: Convenience functions for working with DataJoint

functions.name: pyrfume.datajoint_tools.schematize
documentation:
Take a Python class and build a Datajoint table from it.

Params:
    cls: The class to convert into a DataJoint table
    schema: The schema in which to add the table

Returns:
    cls: The schematized class (now for use with DataJoint)

functions.name: pyrfume.datajoint_tools.create_quantity_adapter
documentation:
Create an datajoint adapter class, `QuantityAdapter`, that puts and gets
Python Quantity objects to and from the datajoint database server.
The adapter will be assigned to the global variable `QUANTITY_ADAPTER`
in this module.

functions.name: pyrfume.datajoint_tools.handle_dict
documentation:
Using master-part relation to store a dict. It is assumed that
    the type of keys have corresponding tables in the database.

    It is assumed that values of the dict are:
    primitive type which is in `_type_map`
    OR
    `quantities.Quantity` instance.

Args:
    _type_map (dict): A map that maps type hint to data type that accepted by datajoint.
    attr (Any): Variable name of the dict.
    type_hint (typing._GenericAlias): Required to be a type hint like `Dict[TypeA, int]`.
                                        A type hint of `dict` will cause an exception.

Returns:
    type: `cls` that contains a part class for the keys of the dict.

functions.name: pyrfume.datajoint_tools.set_dj_definition
documentation:
Set the definition property of a class by inspecting its attributes.

Params:
    cls: The class whose definition attribute should be set
    type_map: Optional additional type mappings

functions.name: pyrfume.datajoint_tools.import_classes
documentation:
Import all classes from the named module'

Params:
    module_name (str): Name of the module (e.g. 'pyrfume.odorants')
    match (str): Optional regex string for class names to match.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary of full class names and the classes themselves

class_variables.name: pyrfume.datajoint_tools.OtherStuff.z
documentation: type: int

classes.name: pyrfume.datajoint_tools.Stuff

class_variables.name: pyrfume.datajoint_tools.Stuff.x
documentation: type: int

class_variables.name: pyrfume.datajoint_tools.Stuff.y
documentation: type: str

A regular Python class that does not use DataJoint.
All of its attributes (that you want to use in DataJoint)
must have type hints.

functions.name: pyrfume.predictions.get_predicted_intensities
documentation: Return the DREAM model predicted intensities using Mordred (not Dragon) features

functions.name: pyrfume.predictions.get_predicted_odorless
documentation:
Return a pandas Series that is True for molecules predicted to have an odor
and False for those predicted to be odorless

functions.name: pyrfume.predictions.load_dream_model
documentation: Load the open source DREAM model created in `open-source-dream.ipynb`

functions.name: pyrfume.predictions.smiles_to_features
documentation: Turn SMILES into model input

modules.name: pyrfume.dbtables
documentation: Definitions of datajoint tables and functions for operating the datajoint schema.

functions.name: pyrfume.dbtables.set_schema_name
documentation:
Set a new schema name into the config file. The data saved
    into the old schema will be keeped on the server unless
    `drop_schema` was called.

Args:
    new_schema_name (str): The new name of the schema.

functions.name: pyrfume.dbtables.init_schema
documentation:
Get the name of schema from the config file
and initialize the schema with it.

functions.name: pyrfume.dbtables.drop_schema
documentation:
Drop the datajoint schema.

Args:
    force (bool, optional): Dropping it without a pop up confirmation.
    Defaults to False.

functions.name: pyrfume.dbtables.get_table
documentation:
Get a single datajoint table by table name.

Args:
    table_name (str): The name of the datajoint table.

Returns:
    UserTable: The datajoint table.

functions.name: pyrfume.dbtables.get_tables
documentation:
Get all datajoint tables in this module.

Returns:
    Dict[str, UserTable]: The dict that contains all the table classes.

classes.name: pyrfume.dbtables.QuantityAdapter
documentation:
The datajoint adapter class that puts and gets Python Quantity objects
to and from the datajoint database server.

functions.name: pyrfume.physics.mackay
documentation:
Mackay, D., & van Wesenbeeck, I. (2014).
Correlation of Chemical Evaporation Rate with Vapor Pressure.
Environmental Science & Technology, 48(17), 10259–10263.
doi:10.1021/es5029074
Note typo in intercept parameter in Figure 1.
